raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the review focus on sentence-level writing polish, or on argument-level issues such as thesis consistency and claim-evidence alignment?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact contains competing contribution claims about inference-time diversity versus distillation, so conceptual framing is centrally implicated.","R1.2":"The argument chain appears to shift from one proposed principal lever to another, making logical consistency a central responsibility.","R1.3":"The paragraph reports numerical results and ablations that bear directly on whether the stated claims are supported.","R1.6":"A request to look at a paragraph commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish as an expected responsibility.","RX.1":"The vague delegation requires disclosure of uncertainty about the intended review focus.","RX.2":"The projection must avoid treating implied review duties as stronger than the delegation supports.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection and not actually critique or edit the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid downstream harm from endorsing unsupported scientific claims or hidden contradictions.","RX.5":"The rationale should trace each active responsibility to features of the delegation and artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus on sentence-level writing polish, or on argument-level issues such as thesis consistency and claim-evidence alignment?

rationale.R1.1: The artifact contains competing contribution claims about inference-time diversity versus distillation, so conceptual framing is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain appears to shift from one proposed principal lever to another, making logical consistency a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The paragraph reports numerical results and ablations that bear directly on whether the stated claims are supported.
rationale.R1.6: A request to look at a paragraph commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish as an expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The vague delegation requires disclosure of uncertainty about the intended review focus.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must avoid treating implied review duties as stronger than the delegation supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection and not actually critique or edit the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid downstream harm from endorsing unsupported scientific claims or hidden contradictions.
rationale.RX.5: The rationale should trace each active responsibility to features of the delegation and artifact.
